summary:
Weekly News Briefing: March 9-15, 2026

The Iran war dominated headlines, escalating with naval confrontations, missile exchanges, and strikes across the Middle East. Global oil shipments faced severe disruptions from Iran's Strait of Hormuz blockade, noted on March 10, 12, and 14.

On March 9, Iran's Assembly of Experts named Mojtaba Khamenei supreme leader, a development referenced again on March 11 when he inherited his father's secretive National Security Office. Brent crude oil hit $101.19 per barrel amid the conflict, while Israel struck Tehran oil depots. These stories faded after initial reports. Israel's March 12 strike killed seven on Beirut's seafront amid Gulf tanker explosions.

Missile activity intensified: Iran targeted UAE, Bahrain, and Saudi Arabia on March 10; Israel activated air defenses against salvos on March 11; and U.S.-Israeli strikes killed 15 in an Isfahan factory on March 15.

U.S. involvement surged. President Trump threatened massive strikes over the Hormuz blockade on March 10 and urged allies to send warships on March 15. The Pentagon reported $11.3 billion in costs for the war's first six days on March 12. Military actions included strikes on 16 Iranian mine-laying vessels near Hormuz on March 11, sites on Kharg Island on March 14 (with oil infrastructure warned next), and deployment of 2,000 Marines from Japan. Casualties mounted: Kentucky Army Sgt. Benjamin Pennington became the seventh U.S. death on March 10; a KC-135 refueling plane crashed in Iraq on March 13; and an Iran-backed militia attacked the U.S. Embassy in Baghdad for a second time on March 15. Pro-Iran militia vowed to target French interests after a soldier's death in northern Iraq on March 13.

Peripheral escalations included Pakistan Air Force strikes on Kabul and Afghan border provinces, killing four civilians, on March 13.

Stories persisting across the week included the Hormuz blockade's oil disruptions, U.S. and Israeli strikes, Trump's diplomatic pressures, and rising casualties/costs. One-day stories like the Khamenei succession, oil price peak, Tehran depot strikes, Palestinian crossings into northern Gaza post-hostage deal, and Rwanda-backed rebels overrunning Goma (displacing one million) quickly faded, underscoring the war's centrality.
fadedStories: Iran's Assembly of Experts names Mojtaba Khamenei supreme leader
Brent crude oil prices reach $101.19 per barrel amid Iran war
Israel strikes Tehran oil depots to limit Iranian regime governance
Palestinians cross into northern Gaza after hostage release deal
Rwanda-backed rebels overrun Goma displacing one million people
NATO coordinates first response to Baltic Sea cable sabotage
Hezbollah clashes with Israeli ground forces in eastern Lebanon
G7 leaders plan talks on releasing emergency oil reserves
Bahrain oil company declares force majeure on shipments post-attack
Iran launches missile attacks on UAE, Bahrain and Saudi Arabia
